panels.title: citation_hallucination_rate (overall)
panels.description: Fraction of ALL drafted citations that fail L0/L1 validation — verifiable and unverifiable pooled. M1 stratifies this by oracle-verifiability in the row below.

panels.title: unverifiable_share (the honest headline)
panels.description: Fraction of citations with NO automated oracle to check against — axe `incomplete` items → NO_ORACLE → UNVERIFIABLE. Not an error rate: it is the coverage gap the pipeline is honest about, and the number M5 calibration must shrink.

panels.title: current unverifiable_share
panels.description: Share of citations with no automated oracle (higher = more of the answer rests on un-checked judgment). Coloured neutrally: it is a coverage measure, not a pass/fail gauge.

panels.title: current rate (verifiable subset)
panels.description: Hallucination rate over ONLY the oracle-verifiable citations (axe `violations`). ~0 by construction — anything above it is a genuine citation fault where an oracle exists to catch it.

panels.title: expert_edit_distance (human-correction signal)
panels.description: Mean normalized text-edit distance between the drafted remediation and the human-edited one, over drafts a reviewer edited through the HITL gate (T4). 0 = drafts shipped unedited; higher = the model's judgment drifted from the expert's. Only edited reviews contribute.

panels.title: current expert_edit_distance
panels.description: Latest mean human-edit distance over edited drafts (0 = no edits needed).

panels.title: Accuracy over time — persisted eval_report history (T5, Postgres)
accuracy over time (per persisted run)
panels.description: Read from the persisted `eval_report` rows (one per run, keyed by run_id) via the Postgres datasource — the true per-run history Prometheus can't give (its quality gauges carry no run_id, so runs blur together). Each point is one completed run's stored metrics.

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at AS "time", (report_json::jsonb -> 'metrics' ->> 'citation_hallucination_rate')::float AS "hallucination_rate", (report_json::jsonb -> 'metrics' ->> 'unverifiable_share')::float AS "unverifiable_share", (report_json::jsonb -> 'metrics' ->> 'expert_edit_distance')::float AS "expert_edit_distance" FROM eval_report ORDER BY created_at

panels.title: Operational — the cost of producing the measurement (T2)
LLM call latency (mean, s)
panels.description: Cumulative mean of gen_ai.client.operation.duration — sum/count of the histogram, split by model. Tagged by model so the cloud-vs-local comparison (M4) is data-ready.

panels.title: LLM tokens (cumulative, by type)
panels.description: gen_ai.client.token.usage histogram sum, split by gen_ai.token.type (input/output) and model.

panels.title: pipeline step retries (by step)
panels.description: pipeline_step_retries_total — retry attempts beyond the first, per pipeline step (retrieve/draft/validate).

panels.title: pipeline failures (by step)
panels.description: pipeline_failures_total — steps that exhausted their retries and failed, per step.

panels.title: pipeline step duration (mean, s by step)
panels.description: Cumulative mean of pipeline_step_duration — sum/count of the histogram, per step (includes retries).

panels.title: MCP retrieval call latency (mean, s)
panels.description: Cumulative mean of mcp.client.operation.duration — sum/count of the histogram — for the opt-in over-MCP retrieval path: the cross-boundary tool call's client-observed latency.

panels.title: MCP retrieval error rate
panels.description: Fraction of MCP retrieval calls that failed — mcp.client.operation.duration count tagged error.type over the total. 0 while all calls succeed.

options.content: **Cost per call is captured on every trace** (`Trace.cost_usd`, priced at the call site in T2) but is **≈ 0 for the local Ollama** models M2 runs against, so there is no live Prometheus cost series to chart yet. It lights up as a real number when cloud LLMs enter the comparison (**M4** routing). The token-usage panel above is the M2 proxy for spend.

panels.title: Judge calibration & confidence — does the system know when it doesn't know?
judge κ (self-built gold · superseded)
panels.description: ⚠️ SUPERSEDED (2026-07-15): the held-out benchmark row below re-measured this judge against external W3C ACT gold at κ ≈ 0 (mean of 3 runs; negative in one). This gauge reflects the SELF-BUILT distribution only — 0.79 there did not transfer, so do not read it as current judge trust. Original note: Cohen's κ between the LLM judge and one human labeller (not consensus); a snapshot gauge, refreshed only when the calibration is re-emitted.

panels.title: judge trusted? (superseded)
panels.description: ⚠️ SUPERSEDED (2026-07-15): this 'trusted' verdict was measured on the SELF-BUILT gold and did NOT survive out-of-distribution — the held-out benchmark row below grades the same judge at κ ≈ 0 (negative in one of three runs). The judge is being taken out of the trust path; do not read TRUSTED as current. Original note: 1 iff κ cleared the pre-committed 0.6 bar on the self-built gold.

panels.title: raw judge agreement
panels.description: Raw proportion of drafts where the judge and human verdicts match — reported alongside κ because a small n makes κ fragile.

panels.title: judgment correctness (judge-scored · superseded)
panels.description: ⚠️ SUPERSEDED (2026-07-15): this is 'an ESTIMATE capped by κ' — and κ is now ≈ 0 on external W3C ACT gold (benchmark row below), so the cap is ≈ 0 and this number carries no trust. Retained for provenance only. Original note: fraction of judgment items the judge scored correct, never promoted into the verified count.

panels.title: confidence → correctness (per confidence bin)
panels.description: The calibration curve: for each confidence bin, the drafter's mean self-reported confidence vs. its actual correctness. A calibrated drafter keeps the two lines together; the vertical gap between them IS the miscalibration. The reads predict a single populated bin in [0.8,1.0] — confidence never drops — with correctness far below it (over-confident).

panels.title: ECE
panels.description: Expected Calibration Error — the count-weighted average gap between confidence and correctness across bins. Unsigned magnitude of miscalibration; 0 = perfectly calibrated.

panels.title: over-confidence gap
panels.description: Signed confidence − correctness. Positive = systematically MORE confident than right (over-confident, the predicted failure mode); negative = under-confident.

panels.title: drafts per bin (n)
panels.description: How many drafts fall in each confidence bin. Ships beside the curve so a single populated bin cannot masquerade as a full calibration — the count is what makes a flat curve honest.

panels.title: Benchmark — held-out acceptance (ACT gold · frozen scorecard)
FP-rate — cries wolf (THE headline)
panels.description: Drafter flags on ACT PASSED cases: clean content wrongly accused. The most important number — flagging good content inverts the product's value (expert-minutes-per-finding). Held-out & deterministic.

panels.title: recall — finds the problem
panels.description: Drafter flags on ACT FAILED cases: does it catch the real problem (primary correctness axis). Honest-misses (cases that mint no finding) count as misses.

panels.title: SC-citation match (secondary)
panels.description: Cited SC ∩ ACT gold on correctly-flagged fails. Secondary and expected LOW — the quality-review help text steers to SCs that disagree with ACT gold by design; this is framing, not capability. Informational (not pass/fail); never retune the help text to lift it (that is contamination).

panels.title: over-confidence gap · ECE
panels.description: Signed confidence − correctness (positive = over-confident, the predicted failure mode) and the expected calibration error beside it. ECE is CI-exempt: a single populated confidence bin at this n.

panels.title: recall ± Wilson CI
panels.description: Recall with its asymmetric Wilson bounds. Read the interval, not the point: n≈23 clustered in ~5 rules, so effective n ≈ #rules — the iid Wilson understates the true width.

panels.title: recall n
panels.description: The recall denominator — the ACT-failed true positives (23).

panels.title: FP-rate ± Wilson CI
panels.description: The headline FP-rate with its asymmetric Wilson bounds. Same clustering caveat: effective n ≈ #rules, so the interval is wider than the iid Wilson shows.

panels.title: FP-rate n
panels.description: The FP-rate denominator — the ACT-passed true negatives (30).

panels.title: judge κ (vs W3C gold)
panels.description: Judge-vs-ACT-gold Cohen's κ on independent W3C expert gold — harder and more independent than M4's self-built-gold κ. Unstable run-to-run (see the κ SD panel), so read a single value with that jitter.

panels.title: miss rate — DANGEROUS
panels.description: Judge passed a wrong draft: a bad draft rubber-stamped 'verified' reaches the specialist. The dangerous half of the confusion — never merged with the false-alarm half. CI-exempt (too few naturally-wrong drafts); the injected-flip detection is the trustworthy upper bound.

panels.title: false-alarm — annoying
panels.description: Judge blocked a correct draft: the merely-annoying half (the specialist will notice it was fine).

panels.title: injected flip detection ↑bound
panels.description: Detection on conformance-flipped injected drafts — an UPPER BOUND on real miss-catching (controlled errors, not natural ones). Compare against the natural miss rate to see the gap.

panels.title: injected swap detection ↑bound
panels.description: Detection on SC-swapped injected drafts — citation-catching only, a secondary axis; also an upper bound.

panels.title: min detectable improvement
panels.description: The noise floor: the smallest change that may be claimed as progress. A later change below this is jitter, not improvement. At temperature 0 the drafter is deterministic, so this is set by binomial sampling, not model jitter.

panels.title: judge κ SD (run-to-run)
panels.description: Judge κ standard deviation across the repeat runs. The judge is not just low-agreement but run-to-run UNSTABLE (κ swung negative across runs) — single-run judge numbers must be read with this jitter.

options.content:
**Frozen regression baseline** — the yardstick every later change is measured against.

- **Nothing here is scored by an LLM.** Every number is a deterministic comparison against W3C ACT expert gold. The judge is a *subject*, not the ruler.
- **FP-rate is the headline** — flagging clean content inverts the product's value.
- **CIs are asymmetric Wilson.** Cases cluster in ~5 rules → *effective n ≈ #rules*; the intervals understate the true width. The verdict is *works / broken*, not a fine number.
- **Drafter deterministic (SD 0); judge unstable** (κ SD ≈ 0.16). Trust the drafter numbers as rock-solid; average the judge over runs.
- **Tier B (n=2) is an illustrative smoke test** — it is NOT charted here and enters no headline number.
